title: Where are *___sequent__TOP__x defined?
body:
I'm able to successfully compile and run a Verilog project called [iob-cache](https://github.com/IObundle/iob-cache) using Verilator. I'm now trying to debug it, but I'm struggling to find the definitions of some of the C++ code that has been generated.

In a file called Viob_cache___024root__DepSet_h62dfe991__0.cpp I see several declarations like the following:

```cpp
void Viob_cache___024root___sequent__TOP__7(Viob_cache___024root* vlSelf);
void Viob_cache___024root___combo__TOP__8(Viob_cache___024root* vlSelf);
```

The stacktrace in my debug session shows that it's called `Viob_cache___024root___sequent__TOP__7` and so I was trying to locate the source, but I cannot find its definition anywhere. Is this function being code generated somehow? 

I was the under the impression that the code gen part only happened when calling the `verilator` executable and that all of the generated source was dumped into the `obj_dir` folder before being built and linked with the C++ compiler. However, I only see the declaration of this function in there and not its definition after the call to `verilator` has finished. So I'm confused as to where the definition for this function is located / when it is generated.

Thanks.
